role: Senior Data & Analytics Engineer, Strategic Finance

evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: You'll build reliable pipelines with testing, monitoring, and clear ownership so the business can trust the numbers.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Testing, monitoring, reliability, and data quality standards recur throughout the responsibilities, clearly outnumbering the single velocity-flavoured phrase about prioritising ruthlessly.
evidence.domain_risk: the decisions our leaders make depend on data they can trust
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Errors would affect internal Finance and Strategy decision-making at Linktree, but the JD does not invoke external financial reporting, audit, or regulatory language, keeping the risk at moderate.
evidence.collaboration_width: Finance and Strategy leaders; Finance and Strategy stakeholders
evidence.data_team_maturity: automating the pipelines and workflows that today eat hours of manual effort
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The role is framed around building infrastructure and automation to replace current manual processes with 'You'll own the full lifecycle', indicating the StratFin data function is still being established.
evidence.jd_authorship: You'll design and maintain the data models that power StratFin reporting, planning, and analysis.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical scope (dbt, dimensional modelling, StratFin reporting/planning) shows precise understanding of the day-to-day work rather than generic boilerplate.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: You'll partner directly with Finance and Strategy leaders, translating ambiguous questions into durable data products.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role explicitly serves the Finance and Strategy function as its primary audience, with FP&A-style reporting and planning work.
evidence.autonomy_level: You'll champion data quality standards and help shape how the team works with data.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strong direction-setting verbs (own, champion, shape) and translating ambiguous questions into new data products show the role defines its own approach rather than executing pre-scoped tasks.
evidence.ai_role: You'll lay the data foundations that make it safe to layer AI and automation systems on top.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: The candidate is expected to build data infrastructure that downstream AI/automation systems will consume, matching the ai_enabler pattern rather than personal AI tool use.
evidence.testing_framing: You'll build reliable pipelines with testing, monitoring, and clear ownership so the business can trust the numbers.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing and monitoring are framed as something the candidate owns and builds, with an explicit ownership verb, not just a tool in a requirements list.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: by making sure the people steering the company work from a single, trustworthy source of truth
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Trustworthiness is stated as the primary role framing in the opening summary and repeated across responsibilities ('data they can trust', 'trust the numbers'), reflecting a dominant fear of unreliable data reaching decision-makers.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: automating the pipelines and workflows that today eat hours of manual effort, and building the infrastructure that lets StratFin scale efficiently
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The dominant verbs (own, build, design) describe creating new automation and infrastructure for a function currently reliant on manual effort, not just scaling an existing system.
evidence.work_arrangement: You can work from our Melbourne or Sydney office on a hybrid basis.
evidence.loss_aversion: the decisions our leaders make depend on data they can trust — trustworthiness is framed as the central mission of the role from the opening paragraph onward.
